_comment: Curated Pulse feed. Hand-edited (with Claude's help) from build/source/_chat.txt. 'sharedBy' should match a canonical name from the people registry when possible so the card links to their profile; otherwise it renders as plain text. 'highlights' lists item ids pinned to the top of the Pulse tab. Types: launch | link | event | discussion | intro | ask | doc. Optional 'eventDate' (YYYY-MM-DD) marks events as upcoming/past. Re-curate after each chat re-export: run node build/parse-chat.mjs, review build/pulse-candidates.md, add items here, re-run.

items.title: VoiceClaw Realtime — big update is out
items.blurb: True realtime voice conversation that can direct OpenClaw or Hermes sessions (and your computer through them). Free, much faster now, with a free Mac Companion app on GitHub — running on GPT-Realtime-2.1-mini released just hours earlier.

items.title: Free webinar: the finance side of AI
items.blurb: Cost drivers, planning around ROI, and saving serious money without losing capability — AI spend explained for business and finance leaders (technical folks welcome too).

items.title: Beep Beep TLV hits the App Store
items.blurb: The weekend project that alerts scooter riders before they hit a helmet-ticket camera — now live with Hebrew support and Waze-style reporting of where the helmet police hide.

items.title: Zaviv — a mobile agentic IDE, waitlist open
items.blurb: Five months, ten hours a day: macOS build already App Store-approved, iOS TestFlight opening to external users, and an Apple Watch app that can prompt any coding agent. Waitlist is live.

items.title: The Fable 5 saga — final hours on subscription
items.blurb: The group lived every twist: launch day, the ITAR shutdown, the comeback — and now Fable leaves subscription plans July 8, 8am Israel time. One member's benchmark: a full end-to-end React Native app cost ~$1,500 in Fable credits.

items.title: bulk.dog — AI images in bulk
items.blurb: Generate hundreds of template-based AI images in one go, born from too much back-and-forth with Gemini for mock images. Free credits for ~100–300 images on signup.

items.title: "Does anyone have an agent that summarizes this group?"
items.blurb: Someone finally asked for an AI that keeps track of all the tips flying through the chat. Several members already run daily WhatsApp summarizers — and this Pulse page is the community's own answer.

items.title: Funded project: computer vision for apartment measurements
items.blurb: A PropTech team doing dozens–hundreds of manual measurements a day from building-permit plans (DWF/PDF/image) is funding a custom internal tool — looking for developers who can actually deliver, to the Israeli measuring standard.

items.title: Distribution partner wanted for two live products
items.blurb: "Forget vibe coding — anyone into vibe marketing?" Two shipped products (mentionwell.com and replymagic.ai) need someone to own growth, potentially as a partner.

items.title: Jacob — GoatZone, football prediction on WHERE
items.blurb: Free-to-play football prediction game where you predict where things happen on the pitch instead of who scores. Launched during the World Cup; real-money pool betting planned later this year. Looking for growth/product people.

items.title: Michael Perkins — chess bets to coding agents
items.blurb: Made a big 2020 bet on live chess spectating + betting (an Israeli team now pursues the same idea at pawn-market.com). Now exploring Hebrew learning tools, dev tools, and mobile-first coding agents — looking for the right problem or partner.

items.title: Web app vs native: the great debate
items.blurb: "Stop building web apps people won't use — learn React Native + Expo" vs "React Native is the source of all people jumping from buildings; Kotlin Multiplatform is the standard." No survivors, great arguments on both sides.

items.title: WhatsApp API pricing shake-up
items.blurb: Meta is changing API pricing — bad news if you sell agents on WhatsApp. The group compared Whapi ($35/mo flat, great docs) and WAHA (newly free, some disconnects). One member automated 98% of his father's 8-hour daily order workflow this way.

items.title: Open-source WhatsApp → AI collector
items.blurb: Free tool that collects group messages via WhatsApp Web into JSON any agent can read — stays logged in, can't be banned, runs invisibly on a virtual monitor. (This Pulse page is built from exactly this kind of export.)

items.title: Hermex — Hermes agent as a native iOS app
items.blurb: Use your Hermes agent from your phone like you'd use ChatGPT. Recommended if you already run a Hermes agent.

items.title: Grok Voice Agent Builder
items.blurb: x.ai's new voice agent builder — "this looks awesome, anyone tried it yet?" The follow-up thread compared it to Retell + ElevenLabs setups.

items.title: Isaac's full Claude/Codex agent setup (zip)
items.blurb: After weeks of using Fable as an orchestrator with Opus/Codex sub-agents, Isaac shared his complete agent setup as a zip in the group — grab it from the chat.

items.title: Fable prompt optimizer
items.blurb: All of Anthropic's Fable docs and cookbooks ingested into one tool that spits out a perfectly structured prompt — built because at Fable prices, you don't want to waste a single prompt.

items.title: DeployTLV #3: First Dollar
items.blurb: Revenue-generating members shared how they made their first dollar (or shekel), plus the usual top-tier mingling.

items.title: Scooter camera alerts — the weekend build
items.blurb: "Hate getting tickets for not wearing a helmet on a scooter?" App tracks city ticket cameras and pings you within 100m. The group immediately demanded Waze-style police reporting — already built in.

items.title: Refero — free design styles for your AI
items.blurb: A free library of real product design styles to feed your coding agent so your app stops looking like every other AI-generated site.

items.title: Payments for indie digital products
items.blurb: US or Israel entity? Lemon Squeezy? The thread's answers: Stripe Atlas in Delaware, or dodopayments.com — handles both Israeli and US payments and spares small businesses the foreign-VAT headache.

items.title: Midjourney is going into medical scanning
items.blurb: The image-gen company's surprise pivot into medical imaging — "really interesting stuff," and the group agreed it's a huge piece of news if they can operationalize it.

items.title: Escaping the "Claude says fixed" loop
items.blurb: A bug survived 5 deploys of symptom-patching. Winning advice: revert all changes, start a fresh context, accept no code changes without proof — the fresh session found the real root cause in one shot.

items.title: What's in your Claude Code setup?
items.blurb: The group's stack revealed: superpowers ("changed my life"), compound-engineering, OpenSpec, the exa MCP, Variant for UI mockups, and Vercel's open skills directory at skills.sh.

items.title: DeployTLV coworking at Nabi Yuna
items.blurb: "No more building alone" — recurring coworking sessions kicked off with 15 builders working side by side. Watch the group for the next one.

items.title: Three-finger window switching for macOS
items.blurb: Open-source Mac app bringing PC-style three-finger scrub navigation to macOS, plus clipboard history and local AI commands that analyze your screen in real time.

items.title: Paid: agent-library builder for Zaviv
items.blurb: Short-term bounty contract building a library of production-ready agents (orchestration, tool calling, memory, scheduling) users can activate with zero setup. Remote or a TLV café.

items.title: Variant — "low key one of the best tools in the space"
items.blurb: Design-variation tool that had half the group raving: generate wildly different UI directions, then hand the winner to Claude to expand. Free tier gives 60 fast runs a month.

items.title: Baba News — Israeli news translated in near real time
items.blurb: All major Hebrew news sources translated, deduplicated, AI-summarized, and tagged by political lean — built in a week as a companion to the Baba translation app.

items.title: Codegrid — tame your Claude/Codex terminal chaos
items.blurb: Free, open-source dashboard for running multiple coding-agent sessions without drowning in terminal tabs. "The interface looks fantastic."

items.title: Bar is building PairMee
items.blurb: A marketplace to match and split planned taxi rides — post your JFK-to-Manhattan route and save 50%+. Pre-launch MVP built solo with Claude Code; seeking honest feedback and a B2C/travel co-founder.

items.title: Day project: World Cup → your Google Calendar
items.blurb: Pick your teams, get a custom World Cup schedule dropped straight into your calendar. Built in a day.

items.title: Loops: revolutionary or rebranded cron jobs?
items.blurb: The group's recurring fight. Real use cases surfaced — morning health checks on released features, a Hermes agent that turns Sentry errors into PRs every morning — plus Addy Osmani's loop-engineering article.

items.title: Boker Tov — prayers before doomscrolling
items.blurb: Side project that blocks your other apps when you wake up until you finish morning prayers. Completely free, on the App Store.

items.title: Witzper — open-source Wispr Flow alternative
items.blurb: From the voice-dictation thread: a local-models dictation app that's "faster than Wispr when it works." Needs bug fixes — PRs welcome. Also surfaced: Handy (free) and nvidia/parakeet on Hugging Face.

items.title: Baba — natural Hebrew translation with gender context
items.blurb: 100,000+ translations and thousands of monthly users; the only Hebrew translator giving natural output with gender context. In talks with N12/Keshet for a custom news pipeline.

items.title: Multi-agent code review workflows
items.blurb: Claude and Codex reviewing each other's PRs, adversarial-reviewer skills with phase checkpoints, and whether a third agent adds signal or just noise — one of the group's deepest technical threads.

items.title: NanoClaw out of stealth: turned down $20M buyout, raised $12M seed
items.blurb: Shared by the member who'd joined the company days earlier — the Israel-based agent runtime the group had been recommending as the safer OpenClaw alternative.

items.title: Running OpenClaw safely — the thread that started it all
items.blurb: The group's first great thread: agents with their own WhatsApp accounts making Hebrew phone calls, and a reference-quality security writeup — Docker on an isolated VM, vaulted secrets, a permission bridge. "Treat it like infrastructure."
